brief_justification: Arrives at the exact correct answer (A) with valid structural reasoning; goes beyond surface by formalizing the math and explaining why semantics are irrelevant, but does not explore second/third-order implications like what would happen if divisibility were relaxed; no explicit naming of own biases or assumption checks, though it flags the distractor strategy implicitly; provides the canonical 0-1 knapsack formulation as a structural verification but no counterfactual or boundary-case test; presents the conclusion as fully certain with no acknowledgment that the indivisibility assumption is doing all the work or what changes if it's removed; addresses each distractor (B, C, D) and briefly explains why each fails, though without deep engagement; maps all three problems to the same formula step-by-step, making the reasoning reproducible, though intermediate steps are compact.
